speaker: CHARLES
text: These news, my lords, may cheer our drooping spirits:

speaker: CHARLES
text: ’Tis said the stout Parisians do revolt

speaker: CHARLES
text: And turn again unto the warlike French.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Then march to Paris, royal Charles of France,

speaker: ALANSON
text: And keep not back your powers in dalliance.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Peace be amongst them if they turn to us;

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Else ruin combat with their palaces!

speaker: SCOUT
text: Success unto our valiant general,

speaker: SCOUT
text: And happiness to his accomplices.

speaker: CHARLES
text: What tidings send our scouts? I prithee speak.

speaker: SCOUT
text: The English army that divided was

speaker: SCOUT
text: Into two parties is now conjoined in one,

speaker: SCOUT
text: And means to give you battle presently.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Somewhat too sudden, sirs, the warning is,

speaker: CHARLES
text: But we will presently provide for them.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: I trust the ghost of Talbot is not there.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Now he is gone, my lord, you need not fear.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Of all base passions, fear is most accursed.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Command the conquest, Charles, it shall be thine;

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Let Henry fret and all the world repine.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Then on, my lords, and France be fortunate!
